steps.title: Read the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence one says Miyake discovered the carbon-14 spike. Sentence two says the event was named a "Miyake event" in honor of its discoverer. Naming a discovery after the person who found it is an appropriate, expected outcome of the first sentence.

steps.title: Pick the transition that signals 'appropriately so'
steps.content: A transition that marks the naming as a fitting consequence of the discovery is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Fittingly,': marks the naming as an appropriate result of Miyake's discovery. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Similarly,': there is no prior parallel item being compared, so nothing to be similar to. ✗
  • Choice C — 'However,': signals contrast, but the second sentence agrees with and rewards the first. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In other words,': signals restatement, but naming the event is new information, not a rephrasing of the discovery. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
